action.reasoning: Observation: Starting iteration 0 with 10h budget and 30 iterations. Goal is to maximize nav_seek_success on Habitat 3.0 Spot social nav. Analysis: With only ~10h and ~30-50ms per env step, pure RL from scratch is unlikely to converge (paper used 100M steps on 8 GPUs). The literature shows heuristic/planner-based approaches outperform end-to-end RL on this benchmark. My strategy is: (1) first verify env mechanics with a short test script, (2) immediately implement a GPS-compass heuristic baseline wrapped as an nn.Module, save a checkpoint, and submit eval to measure pipeline latency and baseline score, (3) later iterate to improve the heuristic or add learned components. Decision: Write test_env.py, model.py, train.py, predict.py. Run test_env.py then train.py to create checkpoint. Submit eval in iteration 0 to validate harness and get a baseline metric.
